panel.origin: The 'Stars and Stripes' features 13 horizontal red and white stripes and a blue canton with 50 white stars.
panel.mixology: The 13 stripes represent the original colonies, while each of the 50 stars stands for a current state.
panel.examples: The design has been modified 27 times as states joined; the current 50-star version, designed in part by a teenager for a school project, dates to 1960.

panel.origin: The 'Maple Leaf' flag features a single red, 11-pointed maple leaf centered on a white square between two red bands.
panel.mixology: The maple leaf is a longstanding national symbol of Canada, and the red-and-white colors were declared the national colors in 1921.
panel.examples: Adopted only in 1965 after fierce debate, it replaced flags bearing the British Union Jack, marking a milestone in Canada's distinct national identity.

panel.origin: A vertical tricolor of green, white, and red, with the national coat of arms centered on the white band.
panel.mixology: The emblem depicts an eagle devouring a serpent atop a cactus, fulfilling an Aztec prophecy about where to found their capital.
panel.examples: The legend guided the Aztecs to build Tenochtitlan, today's Mexico City, making the flag a direct link to the nation's pre-colonial roots.

panel.origin: A green field with a yellow rhombus, a blue celestial globe, and a white banner reading 'Ordem e Progresso.'
panel.mixology: The motto means 'Order and Progress,' and the stars depict the sky over Rio de Janeiro on a specific date in 1889.
panel.examples: The 27 stars represent the states and federal district; the sky shown is that of the morning the republic was proclaimed, frozen in the flag forever.

panel.origin: Three horizontal bands of light blue, white, and light blue, with a golden 'Sun of May' centered on the white.
panel.mixology: The Sun of May commemorates the May Revolution of 1810 that began Argentina's path to independence.
panel.examples: The sky-blue and white colors are traditionally linked to the cockades worn by patriots, and the radiant sun is an emblem also found on Uruguay's flag.

panel.origin: A gold saltire (diagonal cross) dividing the flag into green triangles at top and bottom and black triangles at the sides.
panel.mixology: Jamaica's is the only national flag in the world that contains none of the colors red, white, or blue.
panel.examples: Adopted at independence in 1962, its colors symbolize the strength and creativity of the people (black), the land and hope (green), and sunshine (gold).

panel.origin: The 'Union Jack' combines the crosses of three patron saints into a single overlapping red, white, and blue design.
panel.mixology: It merges the crosses of St. George (England), St. Andrew (Scotland), and St. Patrick (Ireland) into one flag.
panel.examples: Its layered design reflects the political union of the kingdoms, and it appears within the flags of many former British territories and Commonwealth nations.

panel.origin: The 'Tricolore' consists of three equal vertical bands of blue, white, and red.
panel.mixology: Born of the French Revolution, its colors are often linked to liberty, equality, and fraternity, and to the city of Paris and the monarchy.
panel.examples: One of the most influential flag designs in history, its revolutionary tricolor pattern inspired the national flags of countless other nations.

panel.origin: Three horizontal bands of black, red, and gold.
panel.mixology: The colors trace to 19th-century movements for German unity and democracy, and to the uniforms of volunteer soldiers fighting Napoleon.
panel.examples: Banned and revived through Germany's turbulent history, the black-red-gold tricolor today stands specifically for democratic and republican tradition.

panel.origin: A vertical tricolor of green, white, and red, inspired by the French Tricolore.
panel.mixology: Adopted by revolutionaries seeking Italian unification, the design echoes the French flag that inspired Europe's age of nationalism.
panel.examples: First used in the 1790s, it became the flag of a unified Italy in the 19th century; popular legend ties its colors to the landscape and faith.

panel.origin: Two horizontal red bands framing a wider yellow band, with the national coat of arms set toward the hoist side.
panel.mixology: The coat of arms displays the heraldic symbols of Spain's historic kingdoms and the Pillars of Hercules.
panel.examples: The pillars bear the motto 'Plus Ultra' (Further Beyond), a reference to Spanish exploration past the edge of the once-known world.

panel.origin: A red square — one of only two square national flags — bearing a bold white cross at its center.
panel.mixology: The white cross on red is an ancient symbol, and Switzerland's flag inspired the emblem of the Red Cross in reverse colors.
panel.examples: The humanitarian Red Cross, founded in Geneva, honored its host nation by inverting the Swiss flag's colors for its now-universal symbol.

panel.origin: Nine horizontal blue and white stripes with a white cross on a blue canton in the upper corner.
panel.mixology: The cross represents Greek Orthodox Christianity, and the nine stripes are popularly linked to the syllables of the independence motto.
panel.examples: The motto, 'Eleftheria i Thanatos' (Freedom or Death), drove the Greek War of Independence; blue and white have evoked sea and sky for generations.

panel.origin: A unique design featuring a horizontal 'Y' shape in green that converges the flag's six colors.
panel.mixology: Adopted in 1994 at the end of apartheid, the converging 'Y' symbolizes the unification of diverse elements into one nation.
panel.examples: It incorporates colors from earlier flags and liberation movements, and was the first national flag designed in the post-apartheid democratic era.

panel.origin: Horizontal bands of black, red, and green separated by thin white stripes, with a Maasai shield and spears at the center.
panel.mixology: The shield and spears symbolize the defense of freedom, while black, red, and green represent the people, struggle, and land.
panel.examples: The Maasai warrior's shield at the heart of the flag honors the nation's defense of the hard-won independence achieved in 1963.

panel.origin: Three horizontal bands of red, white, and black, with the golden 'Eagle of Saladin' centered on the white.
panel.mixology: The Eagle of Saladin is a symbol of Arab nationalism and a medieval Muslim leader, shared with several other Arab nations' heraldry.
panel.examples: The red-white-black tricolor is a pan-Arab pattern, and the eagle ties modern Egypt to the legendary 12th-century commander Saladin.

panel.origin: Three vertical bands, with green on the outer edges framing a white band in the center.
panel.mixology: The green represents Nigeria's agriculture and forests, while the white symbolizes peace and unity.
panel.examples: Chosen through a 1959 design contest won by a student, its simple, striking design has represented the nation since independence in 1960.

panel.origin: Three horizontal bands of red, gold, and green with a black five-pointed star centered on the gold band.
panel.mixology: The black 'Lodestar of African Freedom' is a symbol of African emancipation, inspiring liberation movements across the continent.
panel.examples: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African nation to gain independence, in 1957, and its pioneering flag influenced many that followed.

panel.origin: Horizontal bands of green, yellow, and red with a blue disc bearing a golden star and rays at the center.
panel.mixology: Ethiopia's green-yellow-red combination became the basis for the Pan-African colors adopted across the continent.
panel.examples: As one of the few African nations never colonized, its ancient flag became a powerful emblem of African independence and pride worldwide.

panel.origin: A white field with a single large red disc in the center, representing the sun.
panel.mixology: Called the 'Hinomaru' (circle of the sun), it reflects Japan's name as the 'Land of the Rising Sun.'
panel.examples: The sun holds deep significance in Japanese tradition and Shinto belief, and the elegant, minimalist design is among the world's most recognizable.

panel.origin: A red field with a large golden star and four smaller golden stars arranged in an arc in the upper hoist corner.
panel.mixology: The large star represents the Communist Party, and the four smaller stars the united people of the nation.
panel.examples: Adopted in 1949 at the founding of the People's Republic, the red field symbolizes the communist revolution that brought it to power.

panel.origin: A horizontal tricolor of saffron, white, and green, with a navy-blue 24-spoked wheel centered on the white band.
panel.mixology: The wheel, the 'Ashoka Chakra,' represents the eternal wheel of law and dharma from ancient Indian tradition.
panel.examples: The saffron, white, and green are often read as courage, peace, and faith, and the ancient chakra ties modern India to its philosophical heritage.

panel.origin: A white field with a red-and-blue 'taegeuk' yin-yang symbol at the center, framed by four black trigrams.
panel.mixology: The taegeuk represents cosmic balance, while the four trigrams symbolize the elements and principles of the universe.
panel.examples: Drawn from East Asian philosophy and the I Ching, the 'Taegukgi' expresses harmony and balance, a meditative emblem unique among national flags.

panel.origin: A red field bearing a white crescent moon and a five-pointed star.
panel.mixology: The star and crescent are longstanding symbols associated with the Ottoman Empire and the wider Islamic world.
panel.examples: Inherited from the Ottoman Empire, the design has become one of the most widely recognized symbols associated with Turkey and Muslim heritage.

panel.origin: A white field with two horizontal blue stripes and a blue Star of David centered between them.
panel.mixology: The Star of David is an ancient symbol of Jewish identity, and the design evokes the traditional Jewish prayer shawl.
panel.examples: The blue stripes recall the 'tallit,' the prayer shawl, linking the modern state's flag directly to centuries of Jewish religious tradition.

panel.origin: A blue field with the Union Jack in the canton, a large Commonwealth Star, and the Southern Cross constellation.
panel.mixology: The Southern Cross is a constellation visible in the Southern Hemisphere, and the large star represents the federation of states.
panel.examples: The Union Jack reflects British heritage, while the Southern Cross has long served as a navigational and symbolic marker of the southern skies.

panel.origin: A blue field with the Union Jack in the canton and four red, white-bordered stars forming the Southern Cross.
panel.mixology: Like Australia's, it features the Southern Cross, but with four red stars rather than the larger constellation and federation star.
panel.examples: A 2016 national referendum considered replacing it with a silver-fern design, but voters chose to keep the historic flag.

panel.origin: The world's only non-rectangular national flag, formed by two stacked crimson pennants with a blue border.
panel.mixology: Nepal's flag is the only national flag that is not a quadrilateral, its shape derived from two combined triangular pennants.
panel.examples: The two pennants represent the Himalayas and two branches of the royal and political past; the moon and sun symbols express a wish for the nation's longevity.

panel.origin: A red field with a single large five-pointed golden star at its center.
panel.mixology: The red symbolizes revolution and bloodshed, while the gold star's five points represent the classes united in building the nation.
panel.examples: Known as the 'red flag with a gold star,' it emerged from the communist independence movement and was adopted upon reunification.

panel.origin: Five horizontal bands of red, white, blue, white, and red, with the central blue band twice the height of the others.
panel.mixology: The colors stand for the nation (red), religion (white), and the monarchy (blue), the three pillars of Thai identity.
panel.examples: The prominent central blue band, added in 1917, was also a gesture of solidarity with allies who shared red-white-blue flags during World War I.

THE AMERICAS.en: Jamaica's flag is the only national flag in the world that contains none of the colors red, white, or blue.

EUROPE.en: The Red Cross honored its Swiss birthplace by simply inverting the colors of the Swiss flag for its now-universal symbol.

AFRICA.en: Ethiopia's green-yellow-red flag became the basis for the Pan-African colors found on flags across the continent.

ASIA.en: Nepal has the only non-rectangular national flag — it's formed from two stacked triangular pennants.

ASIA-PACIFIC & BEYOND.en: The Southern Cross constellation appears on both the Australian and New Zealand flags, marking the southern skies.
